title: Bakewell pudding
subtitle: The original Derbyshire pudding with puff pastry and almond filling
excerpt: The original Bakewell pudding, as distinct from the later Bakewell tart: puff pastry lined with jam, covered with a rich filling of eggs, butter, and ground almonds, and baked until just set. It is softer and more custardy than the tart version.
sourceNotes: Bakewell pudding is documented in Derbyshire baking literature from the early nineteenth century; the Rutland Arms hotel in Bakewell claims the dish originated there circa 1820. The recipe using puff pastry, jam, and an almond-egg-butter filling is documented in public-domain British cookbooks from the Victorian era, including recipes attributed to Eliza Acton and Mrs Beeton.

recipe.yieldDescription: One 23 cm round pudding (6 to 8 slices)
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Best served warm on the day of baking. Keeps in the fridge for 2 days; reheat gently at 160 degrees C (conventional) for 10 minutes.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan: 170 degrees C; the filling should be just set with a very slight wobble at the centre

glossaryTerms.definition: The original Bakewell pudding uses puff pastry as its base and a soft, egg-rich almond filling that sets to a custardy consistency. The Bakewell tart, which came later, uses shortcrust pastry, a frangipane filling that includes flour and bakes to a firmer, cake-like texture, and is topped with icing. The two are distinct recipes despite sharing a name and a town. The pudding is softer and less sweet; the tart is drier and decorated.

content.text: is a simpler bake than the tart: puff pastry (ready-made is perfectly acceptable), a spread of jam, then a loose almond filling poured over. The filling is mostly butter, eggs, and ground almonds with a small amount of sugar; it sets softly, more like a custard than a cake. Ready-rolled puff pastry from the supermarket works well here.

items.prepNote: ready-rolled sheet or block, thawed if frozen

content.text: Preheat the oven to 190 degrees C (conventional). Grease a 23 cm round pie dish. Roll or unroll the puff pastry and use it to line the dish, pressing it into the sides and trimming any excess. Spread the jam over the base in an even layer. In a bowl, whisk together the melted butter and caster sugar. Add the eggs and egg yolks and whisk until combined. Stir in the ground almonds and the almond extract if using. Pour the filling carefully over the jam layer. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes until the filling is golden on top and just set with a very slight wobble at the centre. The puff pastry will have risen around the edges. Leave to cool on a rack for 20 minutes. Serve warm with cream or vanilla ice cream.
